title: Pipeline Observability, Incident Response, Governance, and Improvement · Stakeholder Delivery Mission
summary: An engineering, security, accessibility, and operations review board. The board needs a defended source-to-user chain, reusable automation, trusted npm and image publication, governed approvals, observable delivery, credential containment, artifact rollback, and expiring exceptions. Planned artifact: Deliver Production Delivery System and Incident Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.
Publish with npm trusted publishing on supported hosted runners using exact repository, workflow, environment, id-token permission, and npm 11.5.1 or later.
Contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Ci Build Once Promote Many
steps.instruction:
project case — A project delivery incident exposes identifiers in logs, consumes growing storage, receives blind reruns, and retains permanent exceptions. Reconstruct first cause, contain authority, restore known artifacts, govern retention, and expire risk. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-policy-exception-continuous-improvement-1imdto". Tailor its delivery-operations.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement, keep Ci Policy Exception Continuous Improvement connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-policy-exception-continuous-improvement-1imdto
  - competency: ci-policy-exception-continuous-improvement
    artifact: delivery-operations.yaml
    decision: policy exception continuous improvement
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: change the event from pull_request to pull_request_target
    retained-proof: event ref SHA workflow ref workflow SHA run ID and attempt
    case-key: 1imdto
    change: |
      metrics: queue-critical-path-failure-class-recovery
      cardinality: bounded
      privacy: actor-and-input-minimized
      retention: risk-cost-policy
      incident: contain-preserve-restore-notify
      first-causal-failure: reconstructed
      credential-response: revoke-and-rotate
      artifact-recovery: prior-verified-digest
      exception: owner-reason-expiry
      drill: quarterly
      improvement: outcome-reviewed
    verify: reconstruct one delivery incident from event workflow runner dependency artifact deployment telemetry audit containment and recovery evidence
    failure: Reject unbounded metric labels, forever retention, blind reruns, workflow cancellation as containment, or non-expiring central policy exceptions.
    repair: Measure useful flow safely, retain by need, diagnose first cause, contain authority and mutations, restore verified artifacts, expire exceptions, and drill.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
